name: LangChain4j
tagline: Idiomatic Java library for building LLM-powered apps on the JVM
seoTitle: LangChain4j: Java Library for LLM Apps on the JVM
metaDescription: LangChain4j is an open-source Java library for building LLM-powered apps. One unified API across 20+ model providers and 30+ embedding stores, with RAG and agents.
overview: LangChain4j is an open-source Java library that makes it easier to add large language models to Java applications. It gives you a single, unified API so you can call many different LLM providers and embedding (vector) stores without learning each provider's own API. If you want to try a different model or store, you can switch with minimal code changes.

Despite the name, it is not a Java port of the Python LangChain project. It is built from the ground up for Java, using familiar conventions like type safety, POJOs, annotations, interfaces, and fluent builders. It ships first-class integrations for Quarkus, Spring Boot, Helidon, and Micronaut, and offers ready-made building blocks for prompt templating, chat memory, function calling, RAG, and agents.
features: One unified API across 20+ popular LLM providers (such as OpenAI and Google Vertex AI), so you can swap models without rewriting your code
Built-in support for 30+ embedding (vector) stores like Pinecone and Milvus for retrieval workloads
A toolbox covering low-level pieces (prompt templating, chat memory, function calling) up to high-level patterns like RAG and agents
Designed around Java conventions: type safety, POJOs, annotations, interfaces, and fluent APIs
First-class integrations with Quarkus, Spring Boot, Helidon, and Micronaut
A large set of runnable examples in the separate langchain4j-examples repository to help you start quickly

gettingStarted.intro: LangChain4j is published to Maven Central. It needs JDK 17 or newer. Add the core library and the integration module for the provider you want to use (here, OpenAI), then call a model.

steps.heading: Add the dependencies
steps.body: Add the core LangChain4j library and the OpenAI integration module to your Maven project. Use the latest version shown on Maven Central.

steps.heading: Call a model
steps.body: Build a chat model with your API key and model name, then send a message. The chat method returns the model's reply as a String.

steps.heading: Explore more examples
steps.body: The langchain4j-examples repository contains runnable samples in plain Java and for Quarkus, Spring Boot, Helidon, and Micronaut, covering chatbots, RAG, and more. The full docs live at docs.langchain4j.dev.

useCases: Building chatbots and assistants inside Java and Spring Boot or Quarkus applications
Creating RAG pipelines that ground answers in your own documents using embedding stores
Adding function calling and agent-style workflows so an LLM can use tools and take actions
Staying portable across LLM providers by switching models behind one unified API
